extra.reasoning: The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) holds on all reachable executions. The only transition that can set sent:=true while opted_out is true is t4, guarded by (opted_out and acc == 3). I analyzed the acc update functions: step_0/step_1/step_2 set acc := (3*acc + k) mod 16 with k in {14, 6, 2} (the triple-nested if-subtract-16 structure correctly computes mod 16 since all intermediate values 3*15+14=59, 3*15+6=51, 3*15+2=47 are < 64). All three constants are even, and 3*acc preserves parity, so starting from acc=0 (even), acc remains even on every reachable state. Reachable acc values are exactly {0,2,4,6,8,10,12,14}; acc==3 is never reachable, so t4's guard is never satisfied and t4 never fires. Every other transition touching sent either requires not opted_out (t2) or sets sent:=false (t1 optout, t3, t5, t6, t7). I confirmed this with an exhaustive reachable-state model check: the full state space is finite (2*2*16=64), only 24 states are reachable, and none violates opted_out implies not sent. No reachable state has acc==3. The 18-event bound is moot since the reachable set is closed. Property holds.
